key_items: Public companies added 43,557 BTC in May ($3.2B) - strong institutional accumulation signal
Bitcoin touching critical support trendline never broken in market cycles - technical support intact
Miners forced to unplug amid revenue squeeze with potential drop to $31,500 - major network stress indicator
reasoning: The market shows mixed signals with institutional accumulation (MicroStrategy, corporate buys) and critical technical support levels offset by mounting miner capitulation pressures and analyst warnings of final bear market phase. Near-term price action likely constrained in $60K-$65K range with elevated downside risk from network stress and weak short-term holder positioning (95% underwater).
risks: Miner forced selling combined with sub-$60K support break could cascade to $31,500 if network hash rate correction accelerates, invalidating the critical trendline thesis.
